name: Scapy (Packet Manipulation Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0); pure Python packet manipulation

auth.notes: Requires root/admin for raw socket access; no API auth

performance.notes: Python overhead limits to ~10k pps; use C tools (hping3, nmap) for high-throughput

agent_readiness.notes: Swiss army knife for packet manipulation in Python. Craft, send, receive, analyze, and decode network packets. Supports 70+ protocols: Ethernet, IP, TCP, UDP, ICMP, DNS, ARP, DHCP, HTTP, TLS, Bluetooth, and more. Sniffing, fuzzing, tracerouting, port scanning. Interactive shell or script mode. Network security testing, protocol analysis, CTF challenges, network tooling. Root privileges required for raw sockets.

gotchas: Root/admin privileges required for raw sockets
GPL-2.0 license (copyleft implications for commercial products)
Python GIL limits packet rate vs C tools
best_when: Custom network tool development in Python
avoid_when: High-throughput packet processing (use kernel bypass or C tools)
Production traffic analysis at scale (use tcpdump/Wireshark/Zeek)
Without proper authorization for network testing
summary: Scapy is Python's Swiss army knife for packet manipulation. Craft, send, receive, and analyze packets across 70+ protocols. Supports sniffing, fuzzing, tracerouting, and protocol analysis. Root privileges required for raw sockets. Essential for network security research, penetration testing, and CTF challenges. GPL-2.0 license.
